element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Destructive automata introduced as core chaos manifestations
Required to open final doors; core progression mechanic
Navigation gates; increases complexity in maze design
Economic progression; gold management becomes strategic
Spatial navigation challenge; increases with world progression
Character-specific tool use; expands tactical options
Sentient final boss; ultimate threat and campaign climax

typical_tension_triggers: Complex maze sections with minimal resources
Activated puzzle nodes requiring coordinated weapon fire
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating intensity with structured relief points; two-level cycles build pressure toward shop breaks that reset resource tension

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden power-ups and gold rewards for exploration; player economic choice drives character strength progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Non-linear character selection creates asymmetric player power profiles; health/speed tradeoffs encourage replayability and tactical diversity

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate introduction; basic enemies and puzzle mechanics establish core patterns; character selection creates fundamental difficulty variance
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating threat variety with prehistoric beasts; maze complexity increases; resource management becomes critical before shop cycles
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-intensity automata and creature combinations; minimal gold availability; puzzle solutions require precision coordination
notable_spikes: World 2-3 transition: introduction of prehistoric beasts alongside automata
World 4: Chaos Engine final confrontation; no further shop opportunity to reset health

notable_level_patterns.description: Every two levels (2, 6, 10, 14) provide shop access for upgrades; creates predictable relief windows and forces strategic resource allocation between levels
Nodes activated by weapon fire must be hit to unlock final doors; creates bottleneck challenges and rewards learning enemy positions relative to puzzle geometry
Labyrinthine level design combines resource discovery with enemy encounter pressure; backtracking required to map and explore for gold/keys
Fast characters (Gentleman, Preacher) with low health vs. slow characters (Navvie, Thug) with high health create self-selected difficulty; mid-tier options bridge playstyles
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Persistent across all levels; affects entire run
